examples.code:
# String Interning and Memory Optimization
import sys
a = sys.intern('custom_string_identifier')
b = sys.intern('custom_string_identifier')
print(a is b)  # True: guaranteed exact same memory pointer
examples.explanation: sys.intern() forces Python to enter strings into an internal global lookup table, ensuring single-instance allocation and allowing O(1) pointer comparison (`is`) instead of O(N) string equality comparison (`==`).
